title: Andruris khasiana
intro: Anduris khasiana, also known as Androsace khasiana, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Anduris khasiana include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Anduris khasiana is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or syrup, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and fever.
taxonomy: Andruris khasiana is classified within the family Gesneriaceae. It is a genus within the family, but the specific classification of the genus Andruris is still under research. The family Gesneriaceae is a diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the African violet family. Gesneriaceae includes around 1800 species, which are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Further studies are required to accurately classify Andruris khasiana within the broader plant taxonomy.
